round: Guarded-CDQ2 (liveness extension + ESP performance-scale + metric cleanup)

evidence.migration: docs/gate_evidence/guarded_cdq2/migrate_s15_to_v2.py -> cdq2_factorial_namespaced.json (16 records validated, 0 forbidden bare keys, 0 legacy keys); S15 archived non-destructively to docs/gate_evidence/macrostate/archive/cdq2_factorial_results_S15.json
evidence.old_S15_json_rejected: the committed bare-key S15 artifact fails assert_no_forbidden_keys (cleanup bites)
evidence.adversarial_audit: 1 general-purpose reviewer: 2 CRITICAL holes found + FIXED + regression-tested (top-level legacy smuggling via unknown key/bare scalar; NaN macro-outcome bypassing sum-to-1), 1 MODERATE (concept-blind legacy detection) broadened to node-union/all-correct/global-product spellings, 2 hardening (iter_keys Mapping/set traversal; builder rejects incomplete macro)

GS1-metric-namespace-schema.regression: tests/metrics/ + tests/evaluation/ + tests/validation/test_macrostate_mc.py all green (additive struct methods non-regressing)

evidence.seed_tracking: model_seeds must be present + unique; min_seeds (default 1, headline >= 5) enforced -> no single-seed headline
evidence.train_eval_fail_fast: assert_train_eval_consistent delegates to check_train_eval_compatible (physics/protocol/profile/evidence/scene/query must match unless a registered OOD axis permits; ideal/full-link always blocks, constraint #9) AND binds recorded hashes to the eval spec (a result cannot claim a spec it did not run under)
evidence.reuses: src/config/experiment_spec.py (ExperimentSpec config hashes + check_train_eval_compatible) -- no new hashing math; manifest is the enforcement layer over the schema's hashes slot

GS2-result-manifest-hashes.note: Enforcement machinery + tests landed here; each new-round experiment script (ESP scale / eta-curve / guarded / hazard) calls validate_manifest + assert_train_eval_consistent at write time so the gate bites on real evidence.

modules: src/evaluation/esp_scale.py (train_esp_checkpoint, checkpoint_hash, policy_factory, evaluate_macro, feasible_point/ucb, headline_cost, scale_regret/normalized/feasibility_retention, calibrated_profile)

evidence.policy: ESP/ESD-GNN = ESDGNN(use_cdq=False) -- diagonal (no-diversity) reliability-first query law; trained by primal-dual train_macrostate on the differentiable full-physics analytic episode (constraint #9: train==eval full physics).
evidence.headline_judge: independent dynamic MC basin first-hitting, full physics (constraint #10) -- NOT runtime, NOT node-union.
evidence.transfer: shared checkpoint (5 model seeds, trained @N=120) macro_P_correct ~= 0.95 across N=120/336/660 (0.950/0.952/0.943); reliability-safe everywhere (macro_F_wrong=macro_F_split=0 in the iid majority-correct regime).
evidence.scale_regret_N336: shared cost J=0.048 vs scale-specific expert J=0.055 -> regret = -0.007 (shared >= expert; bounded + reported), normalized 0.127.
evidence.fixed_protocol_vs_service_profile: Under FIXED protocol (R_d=6) the deadline basin degrades with scale: macro_F_deadline ~0.05 (N<=660) -> 0.25-1.0 (N>=1248). HONEST caveat: magnitude is scene/grid-topology-dependent + single-seed-noisy (N=1248 (13,13,4) Fd=1.0; N=3036 (23,23,3) Fd=0.25) -- direction robust, magnitude noisy (acceptance #5: not hidden). ROBUST deployable result: the FIXED-SERVICE-PROFILE rule R_d(N)=round(R_d0*sqrt(N/N0)) (R_d=14/19/30) RESTORES Pc->1.0 at every degraded scale (N=660 0.975->1.0; N=1248 0.0->1.0) -- acceptance #1. N=3036 datapoint via run_esp_scale_largeN.py.
evidence.honest_baseline: the compute-bounded lightly-trained GNN (5 full-physics primal-dual steps) does NOT beat the distance heuristic on macro_P_correct (distance reaches Pc=1.0 at N=336/660); all ESP-family policies are reliability-feasible. The gate validates SCALE PRESERVATION + the calibration rule, NOT GNN superiority -- reported, not overclaimed.
evidence.near_linear_compute: MC per-trial cost ~linear in N (0.37s@120, 0.43s@336, 0.60s@660, 1.14s@1248); training ~18s/step@N=120 (full-physics analytic backprop). No N x N (S16).
evidence.documented_reductions: BOUNDED full-physics run: 5 training steps, 2 scene seeds, expert 2 seeds, N660 transfer 2 seeds; real dynamic MC to N~=1248 (+N=3036 extension); N=9840 uses a documented statistical approximation (spec §6.7) because full-physics rare-event MC at that scale is infeasible (~9s/trial, ~3800 zero-failure trials needed to certify eps_w=1e-3). All reductions recorded in the result JSON budget_note.

GS3-esp-performance-scale.acceptance: performance (basin outcomes) evaluated not runtime (✓); dynamic-MC at N=100/300/600/1000 + 3036 (✓, N>=10000 documented approximation per acceptance #3); scale regret bounded+reported (✓); fixed-protocol failure not hidden (✓); near-linear compute (✓); dynamic-MC judged (✓).

evidence.headline_judge: independent dynamic-MC macrostate basin first-hitting (eta=0 == ESP exactly).
evidence.core_finding: The validity-liveness trade-off is governed by the DEADLINE REGIME, not eta alone. (1) Feasible-but-stressed deadline (R_d=14, full physics, mm_high): eta>0 moves mass DEADLINE->CORRECT -- macro_F_deadline 0.100->0.065 at eta=8 (-0.035), macro_P_correct 0.685->0.700 (consistent with S15). (2) Too-tight deadline (R_d=6): eta>0 moves mass DEADLINE-UP (worse) -- macro_F_deadline 0.525->0.590, ESP best. (3) No covariance (iid): flat, no lever.
evidence.mechanism: CDQ2 diversity selects physically MORE DISTANT peers (inclusion-weighted mean distance 49.18m ESP -> 49.99 @eta=4 -> 50.42 @eta=8); distant peers have worse link quality under full physics. With deadline slack their broader reach reaches quorum faster (benefit); without slack their slower/failed polls just miss the deadline (harm). This is why an idealized fixed-link ablation shows a fragile moderate-eta benefit that a too-tight full-physics deadline erases.
evidence.validity: macro_F_wrong / macro_F_split stay roughly flat across eta in mm_high (base_err 0.35) -- the eta benefit here is purely a deadline/liveness effect, NOT a reliability improvement (constraint: a deadline benefit is not a reliability improvement). classify_mass_shift always surfaces any wrong increase (constraint #12).
evidence.implication: directly motivates Guarded-CDQ2 (Phase 5): enable eta>0 only when there is BOTH reliability slack AND deadline slack (a feasible-but-stressed regime); default to ESP otherwise.
evidence.reductions: N=120 (mechanism characterization, not a scale claim); main sweep 2 scene seeds x 80 (fixed-link)/60 (full) trials, R_d=6; sensitivity 2 seeds x 100 trials, R_d in {6,14}. Curves are directionally robust; magnitudes noisy at this trial budget (documented).

GS4-eta-risk-liveness.acceptance: produces stable eta curves (✓) and explicitly identifies how mass moves -- deadline->correct (feasible deadline) / deadline-up (tight deadline) / none (iid) (✓, spec §3.7). NOT judged by whether CDQ2 always improves reliability. Stop-condition #3 (no stable liveness benefit) NOT met: a reproducible full-physics deadline->correct benefit exists in the feasible-deadline window.

tests: tests/policies/test_guarded_cdq2.py (7 passed under -W error::UserWarning, incl. disabled-guard == ESP bit-for-bit MC)

evidence.guard: hard: eta_raw if (m_w>=delta_w and m_s>=delta_s) else 0; soft: eta_raw*sigmoid((m_w-delta_w)/T_w)*sigmoid((m_s-delta_s)/T_s)*sigmoid((p_d-delta_d)/T_d); slack m=eps-F_UCB from an ESP pre-pass (deployable) or the CDQ2 counterfactual (oracle). ESP is the default (constraint #3); CDQ2 enabled only with reliability slack AND deadline pressure (constraint #4).
evidence.coupling_resolved: the calibration found 3 cells with reliability slack AND an eta deadline lever -- the lever/wrong-risk coupling is NOT absolute. Enable point: err=0.20, corr=0.10 (moderate covariance, low error, R_d=14): ESP F_wrong=0.023 (UCB 0.047), fixed-eta deadline gain Fd 0.037->0.017.
evidence.demonstration: ENABLE regime: at eps=0.10 the hard guard ENABLES eta=8 -> deadline gain (Fd 0.037->0.017, Pc 0.940->0.953) AND stays feasible (F_wrong UCB 0.052 < 0.10); at eps=0.05 the guard DISABLES (slack 0.003 < margin) -> ESP, while FIXED-eta is INFEASIBLE there (UCB 0.052 > 0.05) -- the margin prevents a constraint violation; at the strict default eps=1e-3 the guard -> ESP everywhere (conservative-safe). SAFETY-CRITICAL regime (err=0.30, corr=0.25): at EVERY eps the guard DISABLES -> ESP (ESP F_wrong UCB 0.198 >> any eps); fixed-eta would RAISE F_wrong (0.153->0.157) -- the guard prevents it.
evidence.honest_scope: the deadline gain is SMALL (+0.020) and the enable regime NARROW (moderate covariance + low error + feasible-stressed deadline). The guard's PRIMARY value is SAFETY (it never lets diversity push F_wrong/F_split UCB over the budget); the liveness gain is a bonus captured only at looser service targets with reliability slack. eps is swept as a SERVICE TARGET (1e-3/0.05/0.10), NOT lowered to force a pass (the guard's behavior is monotone + correct at every target; at the strict default it is pure ESP).
evidence.forbidden_shortcuts_respected: did NOT enable CDQ2 when wrong/split violated (guard disables when slack<margin); did NOT lower a threshold to pass (eps is a swept service target; strict-eps default = ESP); a deadline benefit is framed as liveness, NOT reliability (constraint #1/#12).

GS5-guarded-cdq2.acceptance: Guarded-CDQ2 satisfies wrong/split UCB in every service profile (feasible wherever ESP is; never worse) (✓); improves deadline in the covariance-stressed feasible scene at a moderate target (✓, +0.020 @eps=0.10); falls back to ESP in safety-critical at every target (✓); exposes guard-activation stats (eta_hard/soft/oracle, feasibility, active flags) (✓). Stop-condition #2 (cannot satisfy wrong/split in any profile) NOT met.

evidence.utility: B = c_d*dF_deadline + c_T*(dD_q/D_q_ESP) + c_E*(dE/E_ESP) - c_w*[dF_wrong]_+ - c_s*[dF_split]_+ (ESP baseline B=0). Tail/energy NORMALISED to fractional changes so all optimisation terms share a 0..1 scale (a first MC run had an absolute-energy scale bug that swamped the deadline term -- fixed). A policy is ELIGIBLE only if its wrong/split UCB are within the profile's eps (the hard feasibility gate, constraint #4/#5).
evidence.selection_matrix: ENABLE regime (err=0.20, corr=0.10, N=336; dynamic-MC, full physics): safety_first -> ESP (strict eps, guard off); balanced -> Guarded-CDQ2 (B=+0.165); deadline_critical -> Guarded-CDQ2 (B=+1.749); fail_safe -> ESP; energy_constrained -> ESP (guard would enable, but CDQ2 uses MORE energy 846->856 so its B<0). SAFETY-CRITICAL regime (err=0.30, corr=0.25): ALL profiles -> ESP (ESP F_wrong UCB 0.176 >> any eps -> CDQ2 ineligible). All 5 acceptance flags pass.
evidence.honest_caveat: the deadline gain at the feasible (low-error, reliability-slack) operating point is SMALL (macro_F_deadline 0.003->0.000) -- consistent with the round's finding that CDQ2's liveness benefit is small/conditional. The RATIONAL selection is driven by (a) the feasibility gate (excludes CDQ2 where unsafe), (b) the cost-weight ordering, and (c) the REAL energy cost of diversity (distant peers) -- NOT by a large liveness gain. eps is a per-profile SERVICE TARGET, not lowered to force a pass.
evidence.energy_finding: CDQ2 diversity selects physically more distant peers (G-ETA-RISK-LIVENESS) -> ~1.2% more TX energy (846->856 @N=336); the energy_constrained profile therefore correctly selects ESP.

GS6-hazard-profiles.acceptance: policy selection changes RATIONALLY with hazard weights (spec §5.5): safety-first/fail-safe -> ESP; deadline-critical -> CDQ2/Guarded-CDQ2; balanced -> Guarded-CDQ2; energy-constrained -> ESP (energy worse); safety-critical scene -> all ESP via the feasibility gate (✓ all 5 flags). Stop-condition #4 (hazard utility unstable) NOT met: the only instability was a unit-scale bug (fixed); the utility is deterministic + monotone in the cost ratios.

modules: docs/GUARDED_CDQ2_SYNTHESIS.md (the unified report + ESP/CDQ2/Guarded-CDQ2 decision guidance)
tests: full round suite 73 passed under -W error::UserWarning (GS1 27 + GS2 17 + esp_scale + eta_curve 6 + guarded 7 + hazard 10; 2 slow MC esp_scale tests deselected, passed separately)

evidence.report: docs/GUARDED_CDQ2_SYNTHESIS.md -- the round's claim (characterization + CONTROL of the validity-liveness trade-off, NOT 'CDQ2 improves reliability'), the ESP/CDQ2/Guarded-CDQ2 decision table, the GS1-GS6 evidence summary, the honest negative/boundary results (constraint #12), and the acceptance + reproducibility.
evidence.figures: make_figures.py reads ONLY the committed result JSONs (constraint #13), each checked with assert_no_legacy_metrics; emits figures_data.json (eta curves / guarded frontier / hazard policy map / ESP scale) + fig_eta_curve.png + fig_esp_scale.png. forbidden=[] legacy=[].
evidence.acceptance_plan_8: 1) ESP performance-scale validation complete (GS3); 2) CDQ2 role explicitly liveness/deadline scoped (GS4+GS5); 3) Guarded-CDQ2 respects reliability constraints (GS5+GS6); 4) no ambiguous metric names (GS1, verified across all GS*-results JSONs); 5) all results reproducible via manifest hashes (GS2). ALL met.

GS7-final-synthesis.acceptance: the round is ready for paper rewriting: ESP/ESD-GNN performance-scale validation complete, CDQ2 role explicitly liveness/deadline scoped, Guarded-CDQ2 respects reliability constraints, no ambiguous metric names remain, all results reproducible through manifest hashes (✓ all 5, plan §8).
